displayTitle: The Hazard That Travels With You
kicker: RAD SAFETY // CONTAMINATION CONTROL · NR WORK

beats.narration: Gamma radiography is contamination-free by design: the source stays sealed. Neutron work breaks that promise, and radioactive material gets loose.

payload.title: THE HAZARD THAT TRAVELS
payload.sub: A field stays put. Contamination rides out with you.

beats.narration: Irradiated means radiation passed through; leave the field and dose stops. Contaminated means material is on or in you, dosing continuously.

tiles.s: Field passes through; walk away, it stops
Material on or in you, dosing on

beats.narration: In NR work it comes from three places: parts activated in the beam, transfer screens radioactive by design, and the rare leaking californium source.

payload.heading: Where NR contamination comes from
payload.note: ACTIVATION + TRANSFER SCREENS + LEAKS → FIXED / REMOVABLE / AIRBORNE

beats.narration: So we dress for it: taped coveralls, double gloves, double shoe covers. But fabric stops contamination, never the beam — time, distance, shielding still govern dose.

payload.heading: Anti-Cs control the pathway, not the field

beats.narration: And at the line, one rule decides everything. Frisk out, every single time. If it alarms, stay put and call the RSO.

payload.sub: The exit frisk is the only step that proves you are clean.
